words.definition_en: In the state of being active, functioning or operating.
words.example_en: All the lights are on, so they must be home.

words.definition_en: Used before a noun phrase, including a simple noun
words.example_en: I’m reading the book Mary reviewed. (Compare I’m reading a book Mary reviewed.)

words.definition_en: A more remote part of a town or city; the periphery, environs; a suburb.
words.example_en: Many people commute into the business district from the outskirts of town.

words.definition_en: Expressing distance or motion.
words.example_en: Take the chicken out of the freezer.

words.definition_en: Used before a noun phrase, including a simple noun
words.example_en: I’m reading the book Mary reviewed. (Compare I’m reading a book Mary reviewed.)

words.definition_en: A large settlement, bigger than a town; sometimes with a specific legal definition, depending on the place.
words.example_en: São Paulo is the largest city in South America.

words.definition_en: To position or be positioned physically:
words.example_en: Here I stand, wondering what to do next.

words.definition_en: Form of a (all article senses).
words.example_en: I'll be there in half an hour.

words.definition_en: Having given oneself up to vice; immoral; extremely wicked, or sinning without restraint; irreclaimably wicked.
words.example_en: Such immunity to offenders offered a safe asylum to the vilest and most abandoned scoundrels.

words.definition_en: Elongated or trailing portion.
words.example_en: Unfortunately, the leading bridesmaid stepped on the bride's train as they were walking down the aisle.

words.definition_en: A stopping place.
words.example_en: The next station is Esperanza.

words.definition_en: Introducing a clause that is the object of a verb, especially a reporting verb or verb expressing belief, knowledge, perception, etc.
words.example_en: He told me that the book is a good read.

words.definition_en: superlative degree of much.
words.example_en: The teams competed to see who could collect (the) most money.

words.definition_en: An individual who has been granted personhood; usually a human being.
words.example_en:
1784, William Jones, The Description and Use of a New Portable Orrery, &c., PREFACE
THE favourable reception the Orrery has met with from Perſons of the firſt diſtinction, and from Gentlemen and Ladies in general, has induced me to add to it ſeveral new improvements in order to give it a degree of Perfection; and diſtinguiſh it from others; which by Piracy, or Imitation, may be introduced to the Public.

words.definition_en: To deliberately not listen or pay attention to.
words.example_en: A problem ignored is a problem doubled.

words.definition_en: All of a countable group (considered individually), without exception.
words.example_en: Every person in the room stood and cheered.

words.definition_en: The time of day between afternoon and night.
words.example_en: Toward evening, there was heavy rain.

words.definition_en: Nevertheless; yet, still; in spite of that.
words.example_en: He told me not to do it. However, I did it anyway. / I did it anyway, however. / I, however, did it anyway.

words.definition_en: A female given name from Latin.
words.example_en: But the fashion spreads deeper and wider; the village is infected and the village green; Amelias and Claras sweep your rooms and cook your dinners, gentle Sophias milk your cows, and if you ask a pretty smiling girl at a cottage door to tell you her name, the rosy lips lisp out Caroline.

words.definition_en: To move on the feet by alternately setting each foot (or pair or group of feet, in the case of animals with four or more feet) forward, with at least one foot on the ground at all times. Compare run.
words.example_en: To walk briskly for an hour every day is to keep fit.

words.definition_en: The period of time that has already happened, in contrast to the present and the future.
words.example_en: a book about a time machine that can transport people back into the past

words.definition_en: The third-person singular neuter personal pronoun used to refer to an inanimate object, abstract entity, or non-human living thing.
words.example_en: Take this book and put it on the shelf.

words.definition_en: In the state of being active, functioning or operating.
words.example_en: All the lights are on, so they must be home.

words.definition_en: Belonging to her (belonging to that female person or animal, or in poetic or old-fashioned language that ship, city, season, etc).
words.example_en: This is her book

words.definition_en: To do with a place or places.
words.example_en: Do you know the way to the airport?  Come this way and I'll show you a shortcut.  It's a long way from here.

words.definition_en: A dwelling.
words.example_en: And the diſciples went awaye agayne vnto their awne home.

words.definition_en: Used to indicate source or provenance.
words.example_en: Paul is from New Zealand.

words.definition_en: Employment.
words.example_en: My work involves a lot of travel.

words.definition_en: Senses relating to exerting force or pulling.
words.example_en: He drew a sheaf of papers from his bag.

words.definition_en: Near or next to.
words.example_en: The mailbox is by the bus stop.

words.definition_en: The first letter of the English alphabet, called a and written in the Latin script.
words.example_en: Apple starts with A.

words.definition_en: With little or no sound; free of disturbing noise.
words.example_en: I can't hear the music; it is too quiet.

words.definition_en: Inquisitiveness; the tendency to ask and learn about things by asking questions, investigating, or exploring.
words.example_en: It was the first time that the lawyer had been received in that part of his friend's quarters; and he eyed the dingy windowless structure with curiosity, and gazed round with a distasteful sense of strangeness as he crossed the theatre, […]

words.definition_en: The female (typically) person or animal previously mentioned or implied.
words.example_en: I asked Mary, but she said that she didn't know.

words.definition_en: To the greatest extent or degree; completely, entirely.
words.example_en: Thus when they had the witch diſrobed quight, / And all her filthy feature open ſhowne, / They let her goe at will, and wander waies vnknowne.

words.definition_en: To make plain, manifest, or intelligible; to clear of obscurity; to illustrate the meaning of.
words.example_en: The issue was explained to the governor in detail.

words.definition_en: The number represented by the Arabic numeral 1; the numerical value equal to that cardinal number.
words.example_en: In some religions, there is more than one god.

words.definition_en: Pouring with rain; wet; showery
words.example_en: Due to the rainy weather, we decided not to play in the park.

words.definition_en: The time when the Sun is below the horizon when the sky is dark.
words.example_en: Most animals are awake at day and sleep at night.

words.definition_en: Used before a noun phrase, including a simple noun
words.example_en: I’m reading the book Mary reviewed. (Compare I’m reading a book Mary reviewed.)

words.definition_en: Not bright or colorful.
words.example_en: The lighting was too dim for me to make out his facial features.

words.definition_en: Electromagnetic radiation in the wavelength range visible to the human eye (about 400–750 nanometers): visible light.
words.example_en: As you can see, this spacious dining-room gets a lot of light in the mornings.

words.definition_en: The interior or inner part.
words.example_en: The inside of the building has been extensively restored.

words.definition_en: Happening quickly and with little or no warning; in a sudden manner.
words.example_en: Suddenly, the heavens opened and we all got drenched.

words.definition_en: An unsteady flash of light.
words.example_en: the flicker of the dying candle

words.definition_en: In the state of being active, functioning or operating.
words.example_en: All the lights are on, so they must be home.

words.definition_en: In the place of something (usually mentioned earlier); as a substitute or alternative.
words.example_en: I was going to go shopping, but I went dancing instead.

words.definition_en: Expressing distance or motion.
words.example_en: Take the chicken out of the freezer.

words.definition_en: To have a consequence or remnant.
words.example_en: I left my car at home and took a bus to work.

words.definition_en: The female (typically) person or animal previously mentioned or implied.
words.example_en: I asked Mary, but she said that she didn't know.

words.definition_en: An advance or movement made from one foot to the other; a pace.
words.example_en: Turning back, then, toward the basement staircase, she began to grope her way through blinding darkness, but had taken only a few uncertain steps when, of a sudden, she stopped short and for a little stood like a stricken thing, quite motionless save that she quaked to her very marrow in the grasp of a great and enervating fear.

words.definition_en: comparative form of close: more close
words.example_en: In the spring of 1938 the Japanese imperialists invaded Yü-tz'u and T'ai-ku in Shansi. Everyone was in panic as the flames of war came closer and closer to Hsiang-yüan hsien.

words.definition_en: As a coordinating conjunction; expressing two elements to be taken together or in addition to each other.
words.example_en: Soupes dorye. — Take gode almaunde mylke […] caste þher-to Safroun an Salt […]

words.definition_en: To apply a force to (an object) such that it moves away from the person or thing applying the force.
words.example_en: In his anger he pushed me against the wall and threatened me.

words.definition_en: Used before a noun phrase, including a simple noun
words.example_en: I’m reading the book Mary reviewed. (Compare I’m reading a book Mary reviewed.)

words.definition_en: Marked or corroded by rust.

A portal of entry into a building, room, or vehicle, typically consisting of a rigid plane movable on a hinge. It may have a handle to help open and close, a latch to hold it closed, and a lock that ensures it cannot be opened without a key.
words.example_en: I knocked on the vice president's door.

words.definition_en: The third-person singular neuter personal pronoun used to refer to an inanimate object, abstract entity, or non-human living thing.
words.example_en: Take this book and put it on the shelf.

words.definition_en: Physically unobstructed, uncovered, etc.
words.example_en: Come in – the door's open.

words.definition_en: Against.
words.example_en: He picked a fight with the class bully.

words.definition_en: The first letter of the English alphabet, called a and written in the Latin script.
words.example_en: Apple starts with A.

words.definition_en: Not wanting to take some action; unwilling to do something.
words.example_en: She was reluctant to lend him the money

words.definition_en: A low, mournful sound uttered in pain or grief.
Informative.
words.example_en: Lest anyone on the left think that Tucker [Carlson] and his friends are potential anti-capitalist allies, their specific objections to corporate capitalism are revealing. To them, the real issue is not labor exploitation, but rather the “corporate alliance with the progressive left.”

words.definition_en: The first letter of the English alphabet, called a and written in the Latin script.
words.example_en: Apple starts with A.

words.definition_en: An adult male human.
words.example_en: The show is especially popular with middle-aged men.

words.definition_en: In a calm manner; coolly.
words.example_en: She ran to the door and there beheld the ragged street urchin calmly playing his organ.

words.definition_en: To look at and interpret letters or other information that is written.
words.example_en: Have you read this book?

words.definition_en: The first letter of the English alphabet, called a and written in the Latin script.
words.example_en: Apple starts with A.

words.definition_en: A publication, usually published daily or weekly, containing news and other articles. Traditionally a print publication typically printed on cheap, low-quality paper; today usually digital and often also available in print.
words.example_en: Many major newspapers are paywalled because they need the revenue.

words.definition_en: A person who identifies as male, or animal already known or implied.
words.example_en: That friend of your cousin's, he's on the phone.

words.definition_en: To try to see, to pay attention to with one’s eyes.
words.example_en: They kept looking at me.

words.definition_en: Indicating movement towards or location at a higher place or position.
words.example_en: I looked up and saw the airplane overhead.

words.definition_en: To such an extent or degree; to the same extent or degree.
words.example_en: She regards her kids as (being) as holy as saints.

words.definition_en: Supposing that, assuming that, in the circumstances that; used to introduce a condition that may be (or prove to be) either true or false.
words.example_en: If crossing the road, make sure you look both ways.

words.definition_en: A person who identifies as male, or animal already known or implied.
words.example_en: That friend of your cousin's, he's on the phone.

words.definition_en: To possess, own.
words.example_en: I have a house and a car.

words.definition_en: As an auxiliary verb:
words.example_en: The dog was saved by the boy.

words.definition_en: To predict or believe that something will happen
words.example_en: when you least expect it

words.definition_en: Belonging to her (belonging to that female person or animal, or in poetic or old-fashioned language that ship, city, season, etc).
words.example_en: This is her book

words.definition_en: As an auxiliary verb:
words.example_en: The dog was saved by the boy.

words.definition_en: Near the end of a period of time.
words.example_en: The seedlings appeared to be coming along nicely until a late frost killed them.

words.definition_en: A person who identifies as male, or animal already known or implied.
words.example_en: That friend of your cousin's, he's on the phone.

words.definition_en: To pronounce.
words.example_en: Please say your name slowly and clearly.

words.definition_en: simple past and past participle of confuse
Thus far; up to the present; up to some unspecified time.
words.example_en: I haven't finished yet.

words.definition_en: In a strange or coincidental manner.
words.example_en: Though I'd never gone to Boston before, everything looked strangely familiar.

words.definition_en: Not afraid.
words.example_en: Every childhood seems to have exactly such a juvenile in its midst and mists. He's the boy who refuses to fear the opposite sex, purely because everyone else embraces that particular fear, and he's the type who is unafraid to make a decision.

words.definition_en: A female given name from Latin.
words.example_en: But the fashion spreads deeper and wider; the village is infected and the village green; Amelias and Claras sweep your rooms and cook your dinners, gentle Sophias milk your cows, and if you ask a pretty smiling girl at a cottage door to tell you her name, the rosy lips lisp out Caroline.

words.definition_en: To become aware of, understand, or appreciate (a fact or situation, especially something which has been true for some time).
words.example_en: He realized that he had left his umbrella on the train.

words.definition_en: The (thing) here (used in indicating something or someone nearby).
words.example_en: This classroom is where I learned to read and write.

words.definition_en: To lose remembrance of.
words.example_en: I have forgotten most of the things I learned in school.

words.definition_en: A stopping place.
words.example_en: The next station is Esperanza.

words.definition_en: Power, strength, force, or influence held by a person or group.
words.example_en: […]ſtrengthed with all myght, thꝛough his gloꝛious power, unto all pacience and long ſufferyng with ioifulneſſe[…]

words.definition_en: Negates the meaning of the modified verb.
words.example_en: Did you take out the trash? — No, I did not. I do not think it was my turn, was it?

words.definition_en: As an auxiliary verb:
words.example_en: The dog was saved by the boy.

words.definition_en: Having given oneself up to vice; immoral; extremely wicked, or sinning without restraint; irreclaimably wicked.
words.example_en: Such immunity to offenders offered a safe asylum to the vilest and most abandoned scoundrels.

words.definition_en: Behind; later in time; following.
words.example_en: I left the room, and the dog bounded after.

words.definition_en: Every individual or anything of the given class, with no exceptions (the noun or noun phrase denoting the class must be plural or uncountable).
words.example_en: All contestants must register for the footrace: we've arranged numbers for them all.

words.definition_en: Finished; ended; concluded.
words.example_en: The show isn't over until the fat lady sings.

words.definition_en: Used before a noun phrase, including a simple noun
words.example_en: I’m reading the book Mary reviewed. (Compare I’m reading a book Mary reviewed.)

words.definition_en: To go after; to pursue; to move behind in the same path or direction, especially with the intent of catching.
words.example_en: Follow that car!

words.definition_en: Any period of seven consecutive days.
words.example_en: Holonyms: megasecond < fortnight < month < year < gigasecond < century < kiloannum, kiloyear, millennium < terasecond < mega-annum, megayear < petasecond < giga-annum, gigayear < exasecond < zettasecond < yottasecond < ronnasecond < quettasecond

words.definition_en: The female (typically) person or animal previously mentioned or implied.
words.example_en: I asked Mary, but she said that she didn't know.

words.definition_en: To come or go back (to a place or person).
words.example_en: Although the birds fly north for the summer, they return here in winter.

words.definition_en: Another time: indicating a repeat of an action.
words.example_en: I enjoyed it so much I went again the next day.

words.definition_en: As a coordinating conjunction; expressing two elements to be taken together or in addition to each other.
words.example_en: Soupes dorye. — Take gode almaunde mylke […] caste þher-to Safroun an Salt […]

words.definition_en: Another time: indicating a repeat of an action.
words.example_en: I enjoyed it so much I went again the next day.

words.definition_en: All; every; qualifying a singular noun, indicating all examples of the thing so named seen as individual or separate items (compare every).
words.example_en: Make sure you wash each bowl well.

words.definition_en: To habitually go to (someone in distress, sickness etc.) to comfort them. (Now generally merged into later senses, below.)
The outer side of a window or door frame.
words.example_en: The building has a one-story rusticated limestone base and a canopied entrance with a doorman beneath an attractive, rusticated limestone window reveal on the second floor and a very impressive and ornate limestone window reveal on the third floor flanked by female figureshttps://www.cityrealty.com/nyc/carnegie-hill/1105-park-avenue/review/7216.

words.definition_en: One more further, in addition to the quantity by then; a second or additional one, similar in likeness or in effect.
words.example_en: Yes, I'd like another slice of cake, thanks.

words.definition_en: A piece of knowledge that is hidden and intended to be kept hidden.
words.example_en: "Can you keep a secret?" "Yes." "So can I."

words.definition_en: A tabular schedule of events with the times at which they occur, especially times of arrivals and departures.
words.example_en: The timetable has been changed several times since it was first announced.

words.definition_en: Used to indicate source or provenance.
words.example_en: Paul is from New Zealand.

words.definition_en: Not possible; not able to be done or happen.
words.example_en:
Antonio: What impossible matter will he make easy next?
Sebastian: I think he will carry this island home in his pocket and give it his son for an apple.
Antonio : And sowing the kernels of it in the sea bring forth more islands.

words.definition_en: The place set for the end of a journey, or to which something is sent; place or point aimed at.
words.example_en: arrive at your destination

words.definition_en: A small document that acts as proof of something, often thereby granting the holder some ability.
words.example_en: I've got two tickets for the match on Saturday; want to come?

words.definition_en: Marked with a date.
words.example_en: The first dated entry in the diary was from October 1922.

words.definition_en: A group, set, or series of ten , particularly
words.example_en: a decade of soldiers

words.definition_en: At or towards the front; in the direction one is facing or moving.
words.example_en: The island was directly ahead.

words.definition_en: As a coordinating conjunction; expressing two elements to be taken together or in addition to each other.
words.example_en: Soupes dorye. — Take gode almaunde mylke […] caste þher-to Safroun an Salt […]

words.definition_en: With little or no sound; free of disturbing noise.
words.example_en: I can't hear the music; it is too quiet.

words.definition_en: One who rides or travels in a vehicle, but who does not operate it and is not a member of the crew.
words.example_en: It is never possible to settle down to the ordinary routine of life at sea until the screw begins to revolve. There is an hour or two, after the passengers have embarked, which is disquieting and fussy.

words.definition_en: What person or people; which person or people; asks for the identity of someone; used in a direct or indirect question.
words.example_en: You're getting married‽ Who to? (direct question)

words.definition_en: At no time; on no occasion; in no circumstance.
words.example_en: I finally finished, and I never do wanna do it again.

words.definition_en: To appear; to look outwardly; to be perceived as.
words.example_en: She is seeming a bit down these days. Her eyes seem blue. It must have seemed to her she was safe. How'd she seem to you? He seems not to be at home. "It seems like rain". "It seems to me (to be) rather sleety."; "There seem to be a few problems."

words.definition_en: Something unexpected.
words.example_en: It was a surprise to find out I owed twice as much as I thought.

words.definition_en: A particle used for marking the following verb as an infinitive.
words.example_en: I want to leave.

words.definition_en: To perceive or detect someone or something with the eyes, or as if by sight.
words.example_en: Pretty soon I struck into a sort of path.[…]It twisted and turned,[…]and opened out into a big clear space like a lawn. And, back of the lawn, was a big, old-fashioned house, with piazzas stretching in front of it, and all blazing with lights. 'Twas the house I'd seen the roof of from the beach.

words.definition_en: Belonging to her (belonging to that female person or animal, or in poetic or old-fashioned language that ship, city, season, etc).
words.example_en: This is her book

words.definition_en: In the end; at some later time, especially after a long time, a series of problems, struggles, delays or setbacks.
words.example_en: Everyone's true colors will be revealed eventually.

words.definition_en: A female given name from Latin.
words.example_en: But the fashion spreads deeper and wider; the village is infected and the village green; Amelias and Claras sweep your rooms and cook your dinners, gentle Sophias milk your cows, and if you ask a pretty smiling girl at a cottage door to tell you her name, the rosy lips lisp out Caroline.

words.definition_en: To cease moving.
words.example_en: I stopped at the traffic lights.

words.definition_en: A sense of awe, astonishment and curiosity, inspired by unexpected events, novel experiences and inexplicable circumstances, sometimes accompanied by surprise, shock or reverence.
words.example_en: And they knew that it was hee which ſate for almes at the beautifull gate of the Temple: and they were filled with wonder and amazement at that which had happened vnto him.

words.definition_en: Introduces a simple indirect question (without a correlative).
words.example_en: Whether we'll be on time I'm unsure.

words.definition_en: Used before a noun phrase, including a simple noun
words.example_en: I’m reading the book Mary reviewed. (Compare I’m reading a book Mary reviewed.)

words.definition_en: A stopping place.
words.example_en: The next station is Esperanza.

words.definition_en: As an auxiliary verb:
words.example_en: The dog was saved by the boy.

words.definition_en: Not normal; odd, unusual, surprising, out of the ordinary, often with a negative connotation.
words.example_en: He thought it strange that his girlfriend wore shorts in the winter.

words.definition_en: As a coordinating conjunction; expressing two elements to be taken together or in addition to each other.
words.example_en: Soupes dorye. — Take gode almaunde mylke […] caste þher-to Safroun an Salt […]

words.definition_en: To start, to initiate or take the first step into something.
words.example_en: I began playing the piano at the age of five.

words.definition_en: A worded or expressed sentence, phrase, or only a word on its own, which asks for information, a reply, or a response; an interrogative.
words.example_en: What is your question?

words.definition_en: Used before a noun phrase, including a simple noun
words.example_en: I’m reading the book Mary reviewed. (Compare I’m reading a book Mary reviewed.)

words.definition_en: A person with authority; authority, ordinance.
The subjective human experience, regarded collectively; human collective existence; existence in general; the reality we live in.
words.example_en: In retrospect, the process of economic globalization has meant the end of the world as we knew it.

words.definition_en: The part of something that faces out; the outer surface.
words.example_en: He's repainting the outside of his house.

words.definition_en: In the place of something (usually mentioned earlier); as a substitute or alternative.
words.example_en: I was going to go shopping, but I went dancing instead.

words.definition_en: To the full or entire extent.
words.example_en: It is entirely up to you where we go, as I’ll be happy with anywhere.
